slots.headline: Compose Beaver Builder pages like an architect
slots.subhead: BeaverMind uses Claude to draft on-brand Beaver Builder layouts from a brief, URL, HTML, image, or Figma frame — as editable modules, not static HTML.

slots.section_heading: Five ways in, one way out: Beaver Builder modules
slots.feature_1_body: Paste a text brief, drop a URL, feed raw HTML, upload a screenshot, or link a Figma frame. BeaverMind reads the intent and composes a page.
slots.feature_2_body: Not raw HTML, not pixel-perfect clones. Claude assembles pages from a vetted library of Beaver Builder row and module patterns — design-system-aware redesigns you can actually edit.
slots.feature_3_body: Extracts site name, theme color, and logo from the source so hero, CTAs, and typography match your client's identity out of the gate.

slots.left_body:
Tell BeaverMind what's off: "make the hero bolder," "add a testimonials section under features," "cut the FAQ."

Each pass rewrites the plan, not the whole page. Your edits in Beaver Builder stick around.
slots.right_heading: Generate in bulk, pick the best
slots.right_body:
Ask for 1 to 5 variant plans per click and compare hero treatments, section order, and copy side by side.

Feed a sitemap and BeaverMind drafts the full set — home, services, about, contact — in one run, with consistent brand tokens across every page.

slots.q1: Is the output really editable Beaver Builder modules?
slots.a1: Yes. BeaverMind writes Beaver Builder row and module data — the same JSON the UI saves. Open any page in the builder and drag, duplicate, or delete like you always have. No shortcodes, no locked shadow DOM.
slots.q2: Does it try to pixel-clone the reference site?
slots.a2: No. BeaverMind does a design-system-aware redesign: it reads structure, hierarchy, and brand signals from the source, then composes a fresh layout from the fragment library. You get something on-brand, not a Frankenstein scrape.
slots.q3: How does brand extraction actually work?
slots.a3: When you pass a URL, BeaverMind pulls the site name, primary theme color, and logo, then threads them through the plan — CTA buttons, dark sections, logo slots. You can override any of it before generating.
slots.q4: Can it draft an entire site at once?
slots.a4: Point it at a sitemap or paste a list of page titles. BeaverMind plans each page against the same fragment library and brand tokens, so your home, services, and about pages feel like they were designed together — because they were.

slots.headline: Ship the site this afternoon
slots.subhead: Drop in a brief, a URL, or a Figma frame. Let BeaverMind draft the plan. Refine it in the builder you already know.
